item.title: Trump officials may be encouraging banks to test Anthropic’s Mythos model
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: The report is particularly surprising since the Department of Defense recently declared Anthropic a supply-chain risk.

item.title: Apple reportedly testing four designs for upcoming smart glasses
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: These glasses are a step back from an ambitious plan that once called for Apple to launch a variety of mixed and augmented reality devices.

item.title: X says it’s reducing payments to clickbait accounts
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: X is cutting back on payments to accounts that are “flooding the timeline” with clickbait and rapid-fire news aggregation, according to its head of product Nikita Bier

item.title: TechCrunch Mobility: Who is poaching all the self-driving vehicle talent?
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

item.title: From LLMs to hallucinations, here’s a simple guide to common AI terms
item.dc:creator: Natasha Lomas, Romain Dillet, Kyle Wiggers, Lucas Ropek
item.description: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

item.title: At the HumanX conference, everyone was talking about Claude
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Anthropic was the star of the show at San Francisco's AI-centric conference.

item.title: Slate Auto: Everything you need to know about the Bezos-backed EV startup
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: Slate auto burst onto the scene in April 2025. Here is a timeline that covers its origins, backers, product, and other new details.

item.title: Walmart-owned Flipkart, Amazon are squeezing India’s quick commerce startups
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Flipkart's ongoing expansion beyond major cities and heavy discounting is raising risks for India's quick commerce startups, analysts say.

item.title: Kalshi wins temporary pause in Arizona criminal case
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission announced Friday that it has won a temporary restraining order preventing Arizona from pursuing its criminal case against Kalshi.

item.title: AMC will stream ‘The Audacity’ premiere in 21 parts on TikTok
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Is this a smart way to build buzz, or just an odd attempt to recreate Quibi?

item.title: Sam Altman responds to ‘incendiary’ New Yorker article after attack on his home
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: The OpenAI CEO's new blog post responds to both an apparent attack on his home and an in-depth New Yorker profile raising questions about his trustworthiness.

item.title: Nvidia-backed SiFive hits $3.65 billion valuation for open AI chips
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: The deal is interesting for a number of reasons, including that SiFive's chip designs are based on RISC-V, not x86 or ARM.

item.title: NASA Artemis II splashes down in Pacific Ocean in ‘perfect’ landing for Moon mission
item.dc:creator: Graham Starr
item.description: The Integrity craft splashed down in the Pacific Ocean off the coast of San Diego just after 5:07 p.m. Pacific Time.

item.title: How to watch NASA’s Artemis II splash back down to Earth
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling, Graham Starr
item.description: NASA's Artemis II mission has traveled farther from Earth than any mission before. Here's how to watch the crew's return landing in the Pacific Ocean.

item.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: This ban took place after Claude's pricing changed for OpenClaw users last week.

item.title: Battery recycler Ascend Elements files for bankruptcy
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Ascend Elements said that it will be filing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in the wake of a canceled government grant and a challenging market for lithium-ion batteries.

item.title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser’s delusions and ignored her warnings
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: OpenAI ignored three warnings that a ChatGPT user was dangerous — including its own mass-casualty flag — while he stalked and harassed his ex-girlfriend, a new lawsuit alleges.

item.title: TechCrunch is heading to Tokyo — and bringing the Startup Battlefield with it
item.dc:creator: Cindy Zackney
item.description: SusHi Tech 2026 is zeroing in on four technology domains reshaping society: AI, Robotics, Resilience, and Entertainment. Expect live demos of humanoid robots, sessions on autonomous driving's software revolution, deep dives into cyber defense and climate tech, and candid conversations about how AI is rewriting the global music and anime industries.

item.title: France to ditch Windows for Linux to reduce reliance on US tech
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: France's move to ditch Windows for Linux is its latest effort to reduce its reliance on American tech giants.

item.title: YouTube Premium and YouTube Music are getting more expensive
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The YouTube Premium individual plan is increasing from $13.99 to $15.99 per month, while the family plan is increasing from $22.99 to $26.99 per month.
